name: Global.UIPattern.ContextMenu.SubgroupParity_Pattern
observations: Universal pattern: ensuring consistent context menu functionality across all similar UI elements
Example: LOG/FBC/RPC subgroups all provide same menu options (Print All, Clear All)
Promotes UI consistency and predictable user experience
Applicable to any hierarchical UI with categorized subitems
Reuse score: 4.7/5.0
Universal applicability: Any tree/list UI with grouped items requiring consistent actions
Domain transfer: Highly transferable to file explorers, project viewers, data hierarchies

name: Global.CommandPattern.Execution.FocusedWorkflow_Pattern
observations: Universal pattern: providing specialized command workflows for specific use cases vs comprehensive workflows
Example: Print-only commands (display) vs Full hierarchical commands (display+process)
Allows users to choose scope of operations based on intent
Reduces unintended side effects from bulk command execution
Reuse score: 4.8/5.0
Universal applicability: Any system with command batching/bulk operations
Domain transfer: Build systems (compile-only vs compile+deploy), data systems (read-only vs read+write), DevOps (inspect vs execute)

name: Global.ArchitecturalPattern.UI.ContextMenuExtensibility_Pattern
observations: Universal pattern: extending context menu systems to support new entity types
Key steps: 1) Add entity type to filter rules, 2) Extend handler methods, 3) Create entity-specific method, 4) Update configuration
Maintains backward compatibility with existing menu items
Example: Adding LOG subgroup support to existing FBC/RPC menu system
Reuse score: 4.6/5.0
Universal applicability: Any plugin-based or extensible UI framework
Domain transfer: IDEs, file managers, CMS systems, admin panels

name: Global.DesignPattern.Testing.ParallelMethodTesting_Pattern
observations: Universal pattern: creating parallel methods for different workflows and testing both independently
Example: process_node_hierarchical_commands() vs process_node_print_commands() with separate test suites
Enables gradual feature evolution without breaking existing functionality
Supports A/B testing and feature flags
Reuse score: 4.5/5.0
Universal applicability: Any system requiring feature variations or experimental features
Domain transfer: Payment processing (standard vs express), authentication (SSO vs traditional), rendering (fast vs quality)
